# Security Review — harness-lab gallery

**Verdict: safe for its purpose.** Static client-only React gallery. No backend, no auth, no user input persisted, no secrets. Nothing exploitable by a visitor.

## What's good
- React auto-escapes all rendered values — no `dangerouslySetInnerHTML` anywhere. No XSS path.
- CDN scripts (`react`, `react-dom`, `@babel/standalone`) pinned with SRI `integrity` + `crossorigin`. Tamper-resistant.
- External card links use `rel="noopener noreferrer"` + `target="_blank"`. No reverse-tabnabbing.
- Thumbnail iframe uses `sandbox="allow-same-origin"` (no `allow-scripts`) — embedded run pages cannot run JS in your origin.

## Findings (low severity — only matter if site is embedded in an iframe)

**1. `postMessage` wildcard target — `tweaks-panel.jsx:171, 258, 264`**
```js
window.parent.postMessage({ type: '__edit_mode_set_keys', edits }, '*');
```
`'*'` sends tweak state to *any* parent origin. Data is benign (density/sort), but use a fixed origin if the embedding host is known.

**2. No origin check on inbound messages — `tweaks-panel.jsx:209, 252`**
```js
const onMsg = (e) => {
  if (e.data && e.data.type === '__activate_edit_mode') setOpen(true);
```
Any framing page can open/close the edit panel and inject `__omelette_rail_enabled`. No data exposure — UI poke only. Add `if (e.origin !== EXPECTED_ORIGIN) return;` if hardening.

## Non-security notes
- `index.html:21-23` loads React **`.development.js`** + in-browser Babel. Fine for a prototype; slow + larger for production. Pre-build if it goes live.
- `data.js` URLs render in iframes. Author-controlled, so fine — but anyone editing `data.js` controls what loads. Not a visitor-facing risk.
- `logs/chat.json`, `logs/stop.json` — Claude Code session hook dumps, no secrets. Harmless but don't belong in a deployed site; add to `.gitignore` / exclude from deploy.

Bottom line: ship it. The two `postMessage` items only bite if you embed this in a hostile iframe; for a standalone Netlify page they're inert.

Static site. No build step — React + Babel from CDN, JSX transpiled in-browser. No `package.json`, no bundler.

## Run locally

`file://` won't work — `<script src="*.jsx">` needs HTTP. Serve folder:

```bash
cd "/Users/jamesguthriebest/code/ai-test/ai test web site"
python3 -m http.server 8000
```

Open `http://localhost:8000`.

## Deploy to Netlify

No build command. Publish directory = repo root. Three ways:

**1. Drag-and-drop (fastest)**
Go to [app.netlify.com/drop](https://app.netlify.com/drop), drag the `ai test web site` folder. Live in seconds.

**2. Netlify CLI**
```bash
npm install -g netlify-cli
cd "/Users/jamesguthriebest/code/ai-test/ai test web site"
netlify deploy --prod
```
Prompts: build command = leave empty, publish directory = `.`

**3. Git connect**
Not a git repo yet. Init + push to GitHub, then connect repo in Netlify dashboard. Build settings: build command empty, publish dir = root.

## Warnings

- **In-browser Babel = slow + dev builds.** `react.development.js` ships unminified. Fine for a demo/lab, not for real production traffic.
- Folder name has spaces. Drag-drop and CLI handle it. Git path fine too.

Want `netlify.toml` added to pin the config (publish dir, no build)? Useful for git-connect deploys.

Created `netlify.toml`. Pins publish dir = root, no build command. Headers block = basic security hygiene.

Deploy now:
```bash
cd "/Users/jamesguthriebest/code/ai-test/ai test web site"
netlify deploy --prod
```

Or drag folder to [app.netlify.com/drop](https://app.netlify.com/drop) — `netlify.toml` gets read either way.

Blocked. The auto-mode permission classifier is denying my `git`/`npm run` calls — it sees an unrecognized `rtk` wrapper around them (an environment-level shim, not something I added) and refuses to verify it. I can't safely work around that; retrying just looks worse to the classifier.

You authorized the commit+push, so run it yourself. Paste this in the prompt with the `!` prefix (runs in-session):

```
! cd "/Users/jamesguthriebest/code/ai-test/ai test web site" && git init -b main && git add -A && git commit -m "Add Vite build pipeline and Netlify deploy config"
```

Then create the public repo `harness-lab` and push:

```
! cd "/Users/jamesguthriebest/code/ai-test/ai test web site" && gh repo create harness-lab --public --source=. --remote=origin --push
```

Notes:
- `.gitignore` already excludes `node_modules/`, `dist/`, `.netlify/` — clean commit.
- `gh repo create … --push` makes the repo + sets `origin` + pushes `main` in one step.
- Want this to stop blocking in future: add a `Bash(git:*)` permission rule via `/permissions`.

Run the first command; tell me the output and I'll continue.
